item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This doesn't really do much with the idea of a compulsion effect that eventually causes hazardous material to be generated - a common trope on the wiki. There wasn't anything here to engage me or make me think twice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
After approximately two subjects , SCP-3076-A enters its secondary stage

This line doesn't make much sense to me - I assume you are talking about pieces of art or something but you've been using Subjects in the context of people exposed.
I also feel like the test with the computer was a little forced - might have been more interesting if it was something that had just happened due to surveillance camera - the connected computer started blaring out new musical compositions through the site intercom or something.
Still, pretty interesting concept, and I am enjoying your connected pieces here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad to see this on the mainlist! Definitely an interesting concept, and I gotta say, this arc of four articles is very impressive to me. It's ambitious, it's interesting, and the three of them that I've read so far all stand on their own. +1, and I hope the upvotes keep coming.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
I came up with this while I was out hiking.

Thanks to Recursive, Edward Buck, and CannedBread for the feedback!

Image credit is here, with permission from Tichodromamuraria. Go check out her other work!

Check out the rest of the Linville Arc!

SCP-3025: Lucidity

SCP-3058: Concavity

SCP-3066: Absurdity
